title: agrimonia parviflora var. incisa
intro: Agrimonia parviflora var. incisa, also known as small-flowered agrimony or incised agrimony, is a variety of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is used in herbal medicine due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Agrimonia parviflora var. incisa are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues.
